Tubes"}},"8":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"36F971E4F13A4AE19B71E64B3BC26101","url":"https://www.qiagen.com/us/products/discovery-and-translational-research/dna-rna-purification/dna-purification/genomic-dna/qiaamp-dna-kits?catno=51304"}},"9":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"9886F94087EF4D4FBC1E8983C43E22BF","url":"https://www.qiagen.com/us/products/discovery-and-translational-research/dna-rna-purification/dna-purification/dna-clean-up/qiaquick-pcr-purification-kit"}}}}
steps: Start with NEB’s Gibson assembly tool Need 700 bp upstream and downstream “flanking regions” of the gene you want to knock out into one of the suicide vectors. Vector Options: pFOK (KAN resistant), pFOKT (KAN and Tellurite resistant), and pFOG (Gent. resistant).
Add pFOK sequence as your first “fragment”, check the boxes for “Vector” and “Circular”, and select “Restriction Digest” as your method.

Choose 2 restriction enzymes (REs). Any REs are okay, as long as there are cut sites for it in the multiple cloning region (MCR) of pFOK. Open the plasmid in SnapGene and check sites. Once you have everything in NEB’s Gibson tool, test out REs to achieve optimal Tm’s for primers.
Add the 700 bp upstream and downstream flanking regions each as their own fragments. Can copy and paste the sequence directly from SnapGene.

Change the “Build Settings” to the Gibson Assembly Master Mix, 15-25 nt of overlap, Taq DNA Polymerase with Standard Buffer. Keep the rest of the default settings. Shoot for ~40-45 nt total length of the primers to avoid issues with amplifying inserts.
The NEB Gibson tool should then display 4 “Required Oligonucleotides” to order so you can amplify the flanking regions via PCR.

Using the IDT DNA Oligonucleotide ordering form estimate the annealing temperatures for your primers. Upstream flanking region forward and reverse should match and the downstream flanking region forward and reverse to match (≤ 3°C). Overlapping primers with the plasmid/vector tend to have high annealing temperatures, challenging to match up. Trial REs to find lowest annealing temperatures for the outer primers by clicking the pen icon on the fragment.
Once “outer” primers that overlap with the plasmid are obtained, save in IDT tab and put them into SnapGene. Actions > Gibson Assembly > insert multiple fragments.

Use a Genome (ex. 43816) as your “source” of the fragment, choose "use as a template for PCR", and pick Taq. In the fragment tab switch between the 2 fragments and copy over your primers from the IDT page.
Load pFOK into the Vector tab, choose linearize with REs, choose the REs you picked on the Gibson assembly tool page.

Return to NEB’s Gibson assembly tool and toggle “Minimum Primer Length” to ensure "inner" and "outer" primers match, input into IDT until Tm's are ≤ 3°C different. After matching up 4 primers, go into SnapGene’s Gibson assembly tool to ensure alignment.
Check on "product" tab that fragments are in alignment. Your fragments should overlap with the plasmid and with each other.
Design upstream and downstream check primers ~700 bp outside desired region to be used for final screen.
You should also see a green box in the bottom right corner that says “Ready for Gibson Assembly.”

When primers arrive, culture desired knockout strain (e.g. TOP52) AND one of the strains harboring the suicide vector you want to use (Jke201 or Ecs17) at 37 °C, shaking overnight. Located in DAR Box 6.
PCR up the flanking regions using the following recipe:

The following day genomic DNA prep the strain you want to make a knockout to use as a template with Qiagen DNA Miniprep kit . (store gDNA at -20 °C ). Heat one water bath or heating block to 56 and one to 70 degrees.
On thermocycler, select AO check, edit step #3 for Tm, #4 for extension time. Tm = fwd + rvs/ 2. Check IDT sheet for extension time. Number of BP x 90 / 1000.
Purify PCR product using QIAquick PCR Purification Kit for PCR Cleanup

Add 5 volumes Buffer PB to 1 volume of the PCR reaction and mix. If the color of the mixture is orange or violet, add 10 uL 3 M sodium acetate, pH 5.0, and mix. The color of the mixture will turn yellow.

Digest plasmid using restriction enzymes selected during step 1 (ex. BamHI, EcoRi). Follow instructions in package, except incubate for 1 hour instead of 15 minutes. Example: 1x rxn = 1 µg of plasmid, 5 µL of CutSmart buffer, 1 µL of the RE, and then H2O up to 50 µL total. Incubate at 37°C for 1 hr. Some REs then need to be heat inactivated for 20 minutes at 65°C (find card or look it up on NEB). Perform 8x and at least three 1 mL replicates to pool together.
Purify digested plasmid using ZR Plasmid Miniprep-Classic . Pre-warm elution buffer in 37 °C incubator.
Thaw NEB's Gibson Assembly Master Mix on ice (kept at -20°C).

Vortex the master mix and then add: Example Calculation: Upstream fragment 681.8 ng/µL, want 10 uL of To get 75 ng/µL, plug in using C1V1=C2V2 681.8 ng/µL (x µL) = 75 ng/µL (10 µL) X=1.1 µL So add 1.1 µL of 681.8 ng/µL upstream fragment to 8.9 µL SMQ H2O
Mix well and briefly centrifuge.
Store at -20 °C or proceed to transformation (recommend avoid freezing).
Incubate at 50 °C for 01:00:00 in thermocycler.
Make Chemically Competent E. coli Can use Jke201 (requires DAP to grow) or Ecs17.
Email Jordan to order primers.
Record concentration using nanodrop.

Gibson Assembly® Master Mix Instruction Manual Primer Design for Assembly of Restriction Enzyme Digested Vector and PCR-Generated Insert. Restriction enzyme-treated vectors can have 5´-overhangs, 3´-overhangs or blunt ends. When vector is linearized by restriction digestion, the entire overlap sequence must originate from the vector sequence and must be added to primers that will be used to amplify the insert. The overlap region of the forward primer for the gene of interest (orange) should line up with the 3´ end of the overhang on the vector’s left arm and extend back until the Tm ³ 48°C (Fig.4A, Left side). This primer also includes gene-specific sequence at the 3´-end (gray). Keep in mind that the restriction site, which was used to digest the vector, will be lost in the assembled product. However, additional nucleotides may be added between the overlap region and gene-specific sequence region to restore the pre-existing restriction site, or to introduce a new, unique restriction site. A similar principle is applied to the design of the reverse primer for the gene of interest (Fig. 4A, Right side). Figure 4B shows primer design for assembly of the lacZ gene and pMAL-c5X, digested with NcoI and SbfI. In this example, the forward primer of the gene has a "C" nucleotide (underlined) inserted between the 18-nt overlap and the N-terminal sequence of the lacZ gene to ensure the lacZ protein is in frame with the maltose binding protein.
Label and culture colonies growing on plates in 5 mL LB with 50 micromolar (µM) Kanamycin (+ 100 micromolar (µM) DAP if using Jke201) Overnight at 37 °C, shaking .

Once sequence is confirmed, grow culture of E.coli containing confirmed plasmid and culture of desired recipient strain (e.g TOP52) in 2 mL LB broth (+ 100 micromolar (µM) DAP if using Jke201) overnight at 37 °C , shaking. Ensure you have autoclaved 22 mm filter membranes for next day
Make LB-no salt plates using the recipe below: Make Fresh Plates Every Time (Use Within < 1 Week).
Create master mix using outer check primers with recipe below: Use TOP52 gDNA for control. Should see band larger than 1400 BP.
Inoculate 50 mL fresh LB (+ 100 micromolar (µM) DAP if using Jke201) with 500 µL of overnight culture.
Wash twice with 25 mL ice-cold 100 millimolar (mM) CaCl2 solution containing 15 % volume of glycerol.
Aliquot 200 µL into eppie tubes and store at -80 °C .
Resuspend in 5 mL ice-cold 100 millimolar (mM) CaCl2 solution containing 15 % volume of glycerol.
Add 5 µL of Gibson assembly reaction mixture to 100 µL aliquot of chemically competent cells.
Heat shock 00:00:30 42 °C followed by 00:05:00 on ice.
Resuspend in 15 mL conical tube containing 2 mL warmed SOC media + 100 micromolar (µM) DAP if using Jke201. Pre-warm SOC broth.
Incubate for 01:00:00 37 °C, shaking .
Streak out 50 µL , 100 µL , and 150 µL onto LB agar plates containing 100 micromolar (µM) DAP if using Jke201 and 50 µg/mL Kanamycin.
Incubate at 37 °C Overnight .
Gently invert to mix and incubate on ice for 00:30:00 .
Let column sit for 00:01:00 .
To elute DNA, add 30 µL SMQ H2O to the center of the QIAquick membrane.
Place each QIAquick column in a clean 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube.
Centrifuge at 17.9 x g, °C, 1 minute to remove residual wash buffer.
Discard flow-through and place the QIAquick column back into 2 mL collection tube.
Add 750 µL buffer PE to the QIAquick column and centrifuge at 17.9 x g, °C, 1 minute to wash.
Discard flow-through and place the QIAquick column back into 2 mL collection tube.
To bind DNA, apply the sample to the QIAquick column and centrifuge at 17.9 x g, °C, 1 minute .
Place a QIAquick column in a provided 2 mL collection tube.
Centrifuge at 17.9 x g, °C, 1 minute .
Measure DNA concentration using nanodrop.
Store at 4 °C for up to 48 hours.
Transfer 1 mL of culture into 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube.
Centrifuge at 21.1 x g, °C, 5 minutes .
Discard supernatant and re-suspend in 170 µL of buffer ATL.
Add 20 µL proteinase K and mix by vortexing for 00:00:15 .
Incubate at 56 °C for 00:20:00 .
Add 200 µL buffer AL, mix by vortexing for 00:00:15 .
Incubate at 70 °C for 00:10:00 .
Centrifuge at 6 x g, °C, 1 minute .
Add mixture to QIAamp Mini Spin column in a 2 mL collection tube.
Add 200 µL of 200 proof ethanol, mix by vortexing for 00:00:15 .
Centrifuge at 21.1 x g, °C, 1 minute .
Discard flow through and place the QIAamp Mini Spin column in same 2 mL collection tube.
Add 500 µL buffer AW2 and centrifuge at 21.1 x g, °C, 3 minutes .
Discard flow through and place the QIAamp Mini Spin column in a fresh 2 mL collection tube.
Add 500 µL buffer AW1 and centrifuge at 6 x g, °C, 1 minute .
Discard flow through and place the QIAamp Mini Spin column in a fresh 2 mL collection tube.
Centrifuge at 6 x g, °C, 1 minute .
Add 50 µL of buffer AE and incubate at room temperature for 00:05:00 .
Discard flow through and place the QIAamp Mini Spin column in a 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube.
Repeat steps 16.18 and 16.19 to increase yield.
Centrifuge 1.5 mL bacterial culture in 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube at 16 x g, °C, 20 seconds .
Discard supernatant and resuspend in 200 µL of P1 Buffer (red).
Add 200 µL P2 Buffer (blue) and mix by inverting. Cells are completely lysed when the solution appears clear, purple, and viscous. No more than 00:02:00 .
Centrifuge at 16 x g, °C, 2 minutes .
Place a Zymo-Spin column in a 2 mL collection tube and transfer supernatant into column.
Centrifuge at 16 x g, °C, 30 seconds .
Discard flow through, making sure no liquid touches the bottom of the column. Return column to collection tube.
Add 200 µL of Endo-Wash Buffer and centrifuge at 16 x g, °C, 30 seconds .
Add 400 µL of Plasmid Wash Buffer to the column and 16 x g, °C, 1 minute
Centrifuge at 16 x g, °C, 30 seconds to elute plasmid DNA.
Transfer column to fresh 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube and add 30 µL warmed Elution Buffer.
Purify digested plasmid using ZR Plasmid Miniprep-Classic by repeating steps 23-24. Pre-warm elution buffer in 37 °C incubator.
Microwave buffer and agarose for 00:00:30 , swirl to mix.
Set up Owl EasyCast B2 Mini Gel Electrophoresis System tank ensuring orange rubber seal is against the wall and label is facing forward.
Run gel to confirm inserts are in suicide vector using recipe below:
At thermocycler, select AO check, edit step #3 for Tm, #4 for extension time. pFOK Tm= 60.85 °C , extension time = 00:02:20
Prepare master mix using pFOK check primers and the recipe below: Use pFOK as control.
Image Gel
Turn on PowerPac Basic Power Supply to 120 V and run for approximately 01:00:00 . Once sample front is close to the end, stop run.
Load 20 µL sample from PCR into wells, ensuring anode (red) is closest to you and wells are at the opposite end.
Load 5 µL DNA ladder in first well (5 kb).
After gel is solidified, remove combs and rotate gel ensuring anode (red) is closest to you and wells are at the opposite end.
Pour into mold, insert combs, and let sit for at least 01:00:00 but no more than 03:00:00 . Use 20 well comb, insert longer side (1.5).
Once dissolved, add 2 µL SYBR Safe DNA Gel Stain and mix well.
Bring to point of boiling twice.
Continue microwaving in 10-15 second increments until all agarose is dissolved.
Take photo of gel and inspect colonies. There will be a single band at 1400 bp if colony has insert in suicide vector. pFOK control should show small band ~300 BP.
Use Blot/UV/Stain-Free Tray. Clean with H2O before and after.
Resuspend pellet in 50 µL LB and deposit on 22 mm filter membranes with 0.45 μm pores (Millipore, Merck) on pre-dried LB agar plate.
Discard supernatant, resuspend in 500 µL , combine donor and recipient samples into 1 tube, spin down for 10.000 x g, °C, 2 minutes .
Place 500 µL from each culture (3 replicates) into sterile 1.5 mL tubes and spin down for 10.000 x g, °C, 2 minutes
The next day, take 3 LB agar plates and put in incubator to pre-dry.
Grow cultures of colonies with confirmed insert in 5 mL LB with 50 micromolar (µM) Kanamycin (+ 100 micromolar (µM) DAP if using Jke201) Overnight at 37 °C, shaking .
Prep and store glycerol stocks of PCR'd colonies for future use.
Wash filter using 1 mL LB, repeating until filter is clear.
Label 1 50 mL conical for each plate. Use forceps to place filter from plate into conical, ensuring to put back of filter on white label part of the conical.
Mate for 06:00:00 at 37 °C .
Streak out 50 µL , 100 µL , and 150 µL (for each replicate) onto LB agar plates containing 50 µg/mL Kanamycin overnight at 37 °C , for at least 16:00:00 .
Incubate at 28 °C , protected from light, for at least 24 hours.
On heated stir plate in 1 L glass with stir bar, add half of SMQ H2O and sucrose.
Turn on spin and heat up to 100-200°C, let sucrose dissolve before adding other ingredients.
Add remainder of SMQ H2O, Tryptone, yeast, and agar.
Autoclave solution. DO NOT ADD AHT UNTIL AFTER AUTOCLAVING.
Once thoroughly mixed, pour ~ 20 mL per plate, cover with foil, and let sit for at least 00:30:00 .

Add AHT, wrap in foil, and mix using magnetic stir plate. From now on, keep plates always protected from light. Foil the agar bottle while you pour and cover the plates with foil while they solidify/cool. Foil the bags or put them in a cabinet/box in the cold room.
After autoclaving, place in water bath at 50 °C and allow to cool for 01:00:00 .
Streak out 100 µL , 50 µL , and 25 µL onto fresh LB-no salt agar plates.
Combine 6 colonies from each set of plates and combine into 4 mL LB (3 replicates). Grow for 04:00:00 at 37 °C , shaking.
Label 16-24 individual colonies on LB no salt plates.
Grow cultures of colonies with confirmed insert in 5 mL LB Overnight at 37 °C, shaking . Do not add abx or DAP
To make SOC broth: Add close to 1 L MQ H2O and pH 7 with NaOH.
To make TAE buffer dilute below recipe to 1X: Add close to 1 L MQ H2O. For 1x combine 200 mL 50x with 9.8 L MQ H2O
Directly add colony scraped from plate to PCR tube containing 20 µL of master mix.
Fill tank to "Fill Line" with TAE buffer, and load samples.
After growing cultures overnight, prep out plasmid by repeating steps 40-41.
Send prepped plasmid for sequencing by loging into Plasmidsaurus .
Select "plasmid" and "standard low concentration". pFOK is ~7 kb. Double recommended volume, for example, send 20 uL instead of 10 uL.
Prepare sample in PCR tube and label as instructed below:
Print out copy of order information and take to Plasmidsaurus drop box located on the 4th floor of the Couch Biomedical Research Building.
After growing cultures overnight, prep DNA by repeating steps 16-16.20.
Send gDNA for sequencing by logging into Plasmidsaurus .
Select "genome", "bacteria", and "standard". Double recommended volume, for example, send 40 uL instead of 20 uL.
Prepare sample in PCR tube and label as instructed below:
Print out copy of order information and take to Plasmidsaurus drop box located on the 4th floor of the Couch Biomedical Research Building.
Repeat steps 41-43.2.
